issues: Top-level image_paths and metadata.image_paths reference different image paths.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate upstream expectation of visual involvement
asset_registry_record shows 1 associated image and registry_passed=true

however, the question text itself contains all key quantities and asks standard kinematics calculations without explicit reference to a diagram, so image dependence is not strongly evidenced from the text alone
multi_step: the problem has two requested outputs: speed after 2 seconds and minimum ramp length
solving requires applying uniformly decelerated motion relations to given initial speed, acceleration, and stopping condition
the reasoning is structured but fairly standard, with limited branching or complex image-text interaction
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with concrete numeric outputs makes final-answer checking straightforward
the quantities are derived from standard kinematics formulas, so solution steps can be independently verified
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream checking

sentence_segments.text: In mountainous areas with continuous downhill sections, highways often have an additional "escape lane" on the outside of the main traffic lane, known as a runaway truck ramp, designed to allow vehicles that have lost control of their speed to safely decelerate away from the main traffic.
A truck, after entering the downhill section, experienced brake failure due to continuous use of the brakes and entered the escape ramp at a speed of $42\mathrm{m/s}$.
It then underwent uniformly decelerated linear motion with an acceleration magnitude of $12\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, coming to a complete stop just as it reached the top of the escape ramp.
The speed of the truck 2s after entering the escape ramp
How long should the escape ramp be at least?
